About Me

Crafting digital experiences with code

I'm Brian Miguel Maglente, a Computer Science student at Cavite State University - Carmona Campus with a clear vision: to grow into a skilled and well-rounded full-stack developer. I thrive at the intersection of logic and creativity, where clean code meets intuitive design. Every project I take on is a step closer to mastering both the technical and human sides of software development. I believe that great software is built not just with skill, but with purpose. Whether it's building a responsive front-end interface or engineering a solid back-end system, I approach every challenge with curiosity and dedication. I'm continuously learning, adapting, and pushing myself to stay ahead in a fast-evolving field. Beyond the screen, I'm someone who values growth — both personal and professional. I'm open to collaborations, internships, and project opportunities that challenge me to think differently and build something that truly matters. If you have an idea worth building, I'd love to be part of making it real.

Featured Work

Projects

01
1 / 7
Web App
Hazardhub
A data management and reporting platform designed for a hazard monitoring and incident response organization. The dashboard enables administrators to log, track, and analyze hazard incidents with severity classifications — high, medium, and low — alongside resolution statuses. It includes dynamic data visualization using Chart.js for trend analysis across reporting periods, tabular incident management with search, filtering by date range and category, and in-line editing capabilities. A standout feature is the Investigation Report module, a structured digital form that guides staff through a standardized incident investigation workflow, complete with cost tracking, body map indicators, witness fields, and a dual-signature sign-off section. Reports can be saved as templates or submitted directly. The platform also supports Excel export via SheetJS, allowing administrators to generate downloadable reports for compliance and record-keeping purposes.
ReactJavascriptPythonMySQLHTMLCSSFlask
02
1 / 2
Web App
Carmona Estates - HOA System
A full-stack web application built for the administrative staff of Carmona Estates, a multi-phase residential community. The system provides a centralized dashboard for managing homeowner affairs across multiple office scopes, with role-based access control distinguishing between super admins, office admins, and staff. Key features include appointment scheduling and status management, resident complaint tracking with resolution workflows, automated email notification previews before sending, real-time data refresh, and phase-based filtering so each office only sees records relevant to their jurisdiction. The interface surfaces live statistics on pending and resolved cases, and all status updates trigger editable email drafts that admins can review, modify, or skip before confirming. Built with vanilla JavaScript on the frontend and a REST API backend, the application handles session-based authentication and enforces data scoping server-side.
ReactJavascriptPythonMySQLHTMLCSSFlask

Trading Bot
